User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the RotationVista application, storing basic profile information managed by an external authentication system like Firebase Authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity. This corresponds to the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

email.description: The user's primary email address, used for identification and potentially for communication.

displayName.description: The user's human-readable display name, which might be pulled from their authentication provider (e.g., Google).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user's profile information was last updated.

UserSetting.title: UserSetting
UserSetting.description: Stores personalized configuration settings for a specific user, including parameters for the rotation generation logic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserSetting entity. This ID should match the associated userId, representing a 1:1 relationship with the User entity. (Relationship: User 1:1 UserSetting)

userId.description: Reference to the User entity to whom these settings belong. (Relationship: User 1:1 UserSetting)

startRotationDate.description: The initial date from which the 28-day rotation schedule logic is calculated.

generateMonthsAhead.description: The number of months into the future for which the rotation schedule should be automatically generated from the startRotationDate.

timezone.description: The user's preferred timezone (e.g., 'Europe/Madrid'), used for accurate date and time display and calculations.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when these user settings were last modified.

updatedBy.description: Reference to the User who last updated these settings. (Relationship: User 1:N UserSetting)

DayEvent.title: DayEvent
DayEvent.description: Represents a specific event or status for a single calendar day, including rotation, travel, or vacation details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DayEvent entity. This typically uses the 'YYYY-MM-DD' format and is unique within a user's collection of day events. (Relationship: User 1:N DayEvent)

userId.description: Reference to the User entity to whom this day event belongs. (Relationship: User 1:N DayEvent)

dateKey.description: The specific calendar date for this event, formatted as 'YYYY-MM-DD'.

dayType.description: The categorizing type of event for this day. Valid values include 'ROTATION', 'TRAVEL', 'VACATION', or 'NORMAL'.

flightTicketPurchased.description: Indicates whether flight tickets for travel on this day have been purchased. Primarily relevant if dayType is 'TRAVEL'.

flightInfo.description: Optional free-form text field to store details about flights, such as flight numbers, schedules, or PNR (Passenger Name Record).

notes.description: Optional free-form, multi-line text field for any additional notes or reminders specific to this day.

source.description: Indicates whether this day event was manually created/edited ('MANUAL') by the user or automatically generated ('GENERATED') by the rotation logic.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this day event was initially recorded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this day event was last modified.

updatedBy.description: Reference to the User who last updated this day event. (Relationship: User 1:N DayEvent)

definition.description: Stores the core profile information for each user, directly linked to their Firebase Authentication UID. Authorization ensures only the authenticated user can access their own profile document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores a single document containing personalized configuration settings (e.g., start rotation date, generation range) for a specific user. This is a 1:1 relationship with the User, identified by a fixed document ID 'profile'. Authorization is strictly limited to the owner user via path-based access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores individual calendar events for a user, such as rotation, travel, or vacation days. Each document's ID (`dayEventId`) is formatted as 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Authorization ensures only the authenticated user can manage their own day events. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for consistency with path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: dayEventId
params.description: The specific date for the event, formatted as 'YYYY-MM-DD', serving as the document ID.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. All user-specific data is organized hierarchically under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, ensuring strong authorization independence and simplifying security rules.  Authorization for all collections (`users`, `settings`, `dayEvents`) is achieved by comparing `request.auth.uid` directly with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules (Authorization Independence), making them atomic, performant, and easy to debug. Because all data is segregated by `userId` in the path, `list` operations are inherently secure (QAPs - Rules are not Filters). Clients must specify the `userId` in their queries, which can then be validated against `request.auth.uid`. This prevents clients from inadvertently or maliciously querying data belonging to other users. The structure consistently uses path-based ownership for all user-owned data, such as `/users/{userId}/settings/profile` for user settings and `/users/{userId}/dayEvents/{dayEventId}` for individual day events, reinforcing a clear and predictable access model. Each subcollection maintains a homogeneous security posture, meaning all documents within it share the same access requirements, further simplifying rule logic. Denormalization of authorization context is handled inherently by the path structure itself; the `userId` in the path provides all necessary context, negating the need to duplicate owner IDs within every document for authorization purposes, although `userId` is present in `DayEvent` and `UserSetting` schemas for data consistency and queryability. Standardized field names like `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, and `updatedBy` are included for auditability and invariants, supporting robust data integrity.
